$comment: c22 (D-PP-LINE-COMMENT-BEFORE-DIRECTIVE): end-to-end pin that a `#` directive immediately following a `code // comment` line is recognized. main() defines ADD via a `#define` that sits right after `int r = 5; // trailing comment`, then returns r + ADD = 5 + 37 = 42. Before the fix the line comment swallowed its terminating newline, so the directive lost its line boundary and leaked to the parser (compile failure); the binary's exit 42 proves the directive was processed AND ADD expanded. No stdout (libc-free); exit code only. arm64-elf runs under qemu; macho on the macOS leg.
